title: WTH is Maris civilian outfit supposed to be
display_title: WTH is Maris civilian outfit supposed to be
wikitext:
[]: WTH is Mari's civilian outfit supposed to be?             Quote Post by MarqFJA87 » Sat Nov 08, 2014 3:07 pm Just a simple question... According to this interview with Sadamato , Mari's rather iconic school outfit is supposed to be "missionary school-style". Is that supposed to be the Japanese equivalent term for "Catholic school uniform"? Alternatively, does Mari's school outfit qualify as a Catholic school uniform? ... What counts as a Catholic school uniform for girls, anyway? I can't figure this out even after consulting Wikipedia, aside from the skirt being traditionally plaid-pattern. It's further complicated by how common it has become to fetishize the outfit design (or any schoolgirl outfit, by proxy), to the point that to many people they think that that is what "Catholic schoolgirl outfit" refers to.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Nov 08, 2014 3:20 pm I found a bunch of examples that looked spot on to what Mari wears. In particular compare her outfit: http://img3.wikia.nocookie.net/__cb20131204192238/evangelion/images/6/6c/Mari_Makinami_Illustrious_-_Casual.png With the image on this page: http://feminspire.com/school-uniform-rules-are-disturbing-and-sexist/ Pretty damn close.

[]:             Quote Post by mechashiva » Sat Nov 08, 2014 8:47 pm Yeah, Catholic school uniforms usually have a white blouse (sometimes a patch or design denoting the school on the breast pocket or a jacket with that patch on the outer breast pocket) some plaid patterned skirt, stockings and some kind of tie, though that appears to be optional. I should ask my Mom. She had to wear a uniform when she lived in Trinidad. *missing the point* British? I thought Mari was American?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Nov 08, 2014 9:05 pm ^ Nope, she's confirmed by the official website to be half-British half-Japanese.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Nov 09, 2014 4:41 am Some time 3-4 years ago we had someone join who lived near a school with a similar uniform. Photographic evidence was not forthcoming, but somewhere there's a link to the outfitter. mechashiva wrote: *missing the point* British? I thought Mari was American?

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Sun Nov 09, 2014 8:17 am ElMariachi wrote: ^ Nope, she's confirmed by the official website to be half-British half-Japanese.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Sun Nov 09, 2014 8:18 am xPearse wrote: I really hate when they do that, is the half Japanese part really necessary? Because, god forbid they can't fap if she wasn't at least part flower. Same with Asuka, but she's only quarter Japanese, rolls eyes. Something wrong having them full blooded foreigners? I don't mean to jump on a hate parade, anyway, what do you guys think?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Nov 09, 2014 8:19 am xPearse wrote: what do you guys think?

[]:             Quote Post by MarqFJA87 » Mon Nov 10, 2014 10:39 am So... If Mari indeed went to a Catholic school before coming to Japan, what does that say of her character? That her family (though evidently not her ) is relatively religiously-conservative, or something? xPearse wrote: I really hate when they do that, is the half Japanese part really necessary? Because, god forbid they can't fap if she wasn't at least part flower. Same with Asuka, but she's only quarter Japanese, rolls eyes. Something wrong having them full blooded foreigners? I don't mean to jump on a hate parade, anyway, what do you guys think?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Nov 10, 2014 10:53 am It's not hard for a non-Catholic family to persuade a Catholic school in the UK to take a child, though religious families would indeed be the norm. Catholics are not necessarily that conservative, though. My sister is married to an Anglican priest, and their daughters went to the local Catholic school!

[]:             Quote Post by mechashiva » Mon Nov 10, 2014 11:20 am If Mari is British (or half-British) she could just come from a more private school, maybe not necessarily religious. I can't speak definitively on that but it seems Britain has more private but secular schools were uniforms are the norm. That would be an interesting detail to add, should they ever go into Mari's background in the next Rebuild movie. Has anybody been able to get a better view on the seal on her blouse?

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Mon Nov 10, 2014 11:54 am ^ Till college we have uniforms anyway however the ones mari wears are usually optional, they can wear skirts but also sell pants (most of our uniforms were similar to the male ones in Shinji's school since those look decent unisex), skirts and the type of patterns mari has on it only really seem to be mandatory in private schools of which there are very few

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Mon Nov 10, 2014 12:05 pm MarqFJA87 wrote: So... If Mari indeed went to a Catholic school before coming to Japan, what does that say of her character? That her family (though evidently not her ) is relatively religiously-conservative, or something?
